title: Phase 3 — mBERT attention + persistent homology
description: Main experiment. Extract mBERT attention matrices, convert to distance matrices, compute PH, extract topological features. Per Kushnareva methodology.

title: Phase 1 — KWIC extraction
description: Extract ~200 KWIC sentences per canon term per language from Leipzig Corpora. Three languages, three domains. Outputs feed Phase 3.

title: Canon term lists from literature
description: Build literature-grounded canon term lists per language per domain. Each canon term must trace to a specific publication. Document any deviations from sources.

title: Color domain literature search
description: Berlin and Kay (1969) basic color terms; post-1969 critiques; Russian blue distinction (Davidoff 1999, Winawer 2007, Paramei). Compile authoritative term inventories for English, Russian, Spanish.

title: Emotion domain literature search
description: Wierzbicka NSM primes; Russian тоска literature (Wierzbicka 1992, Apresjan); Spanish duende and affective vocabulary. Compile authoritative term inventories for English, Russian, Spanish.

title: Kinship domain literature search
description: Murdock (1949) Social Structure; Greenberg cross-linguistic kinship typology; Kronenfeld. Compile authoritative term inventories for English, Russian, Spanish.

title: Phase 4 — comparison and prediction tests
description: Statistical comparison of feature profiles cross-linguistically. Test the four pre-registered predictions. Mann-Whitney U, permutation, bottleneck/Wasserstein distances.

title: Notebook-faithful rewrite of Kushnareva pipeline
description: Port Kushnareva 2021 notebooks and utility files into adapted versions in notebooks/. Preserve structure and function names. Frozen reference at reference/.

title: Port grab_weights.py and verify on toy example
description: First piece of the rewrite. Read reference/grab_weights.py carefully, port to a clean module, verify equivalence on a toy mBERT input.

title: Adapt features_calculation_by_thresholds.ipynb
description: Notebook-faithful port. Preserve cell structure and function names. Adapt inputs only.

title: Adapt features_calculation_ripser_and_templates.ipynb
description: Notebook-faithful port. The PH computation core. Be especially careful here — the ripser zero-output bug in tda-project came from this area.

title: Adapt features_prediction.ipynb to cross-linguistic comparison
description: Their original is binary classification (logistic regression on features). Adapt to cross-linguistic comparison: feature profiles per language per domain, statistical tests across language pairs.

title: Phase 2 — fastText baseline
description: Conventional cross-linguistic baseline using pre-aligned fastText vectors. Cosine similarity, NN overlap, clustering. Comparison point for the topology results.

title: Phase 5 — writeup
description: Final paper, code repo, presentation slides, AI disclosure. Due May 6 2026.
